rationale: Lim (2010) is not only listed in grouped background citations; the citing paper explicitly discusses how its disclosure manipulation differs from Lim's social-pressure manipulation. The authors use Lim as a key benchmark in the nonmonetary contest-design literature to position their own contribution and experimental design choices. This role meaningfully informs the paper's framing and claimed novelty, so the citation is substantive rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the research line of Lim (2010) on behavioral utility in contests by introducing disclosure-scheme design as the focal lever. It contrasts its setup with Lim's manipulation of social pressure and adds new evidence on how public recognition formats interact with prize spread.
